page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 dedicated vCPUs with 4 physical cores and 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.g5.xlarge in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores are 1102.39 and 2936.9 respectively, both falling in the bottom 25 percent. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 164.24 GB/sec, but large block uncached read speeds drop to 21.08 GB/sec, which is categorized as poor. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show poor performance, while Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are 988.0 and 2175.0. Redis SET operations achieve 2.88 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 3.36 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g5.xlarge offers a balanced resource allocation of CPU and memory for entry-level or legacy workloads that do not require high-performance compute or high-speed local storage. Given its weak-to-poor benchmark profile, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small database operations, and development or testing environments where resource density is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling in the bottom 25 percent, and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks categorized as poor. The instance achieves 2.88 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 3.36 GB/sec in static web serving. Given its balanced resource profile and moderate performance characteristics, the ecs.g5.xlarge is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small database operations, and development or testing environments.
